meta.title: Sanokura Ski Area
meta.description: Kitakata, Fukushima — 8-hour lift ticket ¥2,500, quiet north-facing slopes, Kitakata ramen and Netsu-shio Onsen 10 minutes away.

hero.title:
Sanokura
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Quiet north-facing slopes — powder into the afternoon, then a short drive to Kitakata ramen and Netsu-shio Onsen.

live.subtitle: Lifts, snow, and night skiing at a glance

yotei.label: View
yotei.note: Sea of clouds on clear days

transit.title:
After skiing,
ramen and a secret onsen.
transit.lead: Morning powder on quiet north-facing slopes — then Kitakata's famous ramen and Netsu-shio Onsen, about 10 minutes by car. One winter day across Aizu.
transit.imgAlt: Panorama over the Aizu Basin from Sanokura Ski Area

primary.title: No crowds · north-facing
primary.lead: Not competing with mega-resorts on size — almost no lift lines, ungroomed pockets, and stable snow on north-facing terrain. Locals say powder can last into the afternoon.
primary.imgAlt: Wide view of the Aizu Basin from Sanokura

secondary.title: Kitakata ramen · onsen
secondary.lead: Post-ski Kitakata ramen culture and Netsu-shio Onsen nearby — use Sanokura as a hub, not a one-stop hill.

pass.title:
8 hours ¥2,500,
4 hours ¥1,500.
pass.lead: Adult 8-hour ticket ¥2,500, 4-hour ¥1,500, child 8-hour ¥1,800 — strong value nationwide. Third Sunday of each month is Kids Ski Day (child ¥1,000 — confirm locally).
pass.noticeBody: Base elevation 570–780 m — warm winters can shorten the season. Call or check official info before a long drive.
pass.imgAlt: Pair lifts and forest course at Sanokura

rental.title:
Rest house
"Kurara"
rental.lead: On-hill breaks and light meals — hot drinks on cold days. Rental availability varies by season; confirm ahead.
rental.whisper: Cell signal can be weak on the mountain — you may upload photos after descending.
rental.imgAlt: Kurara rest house at the base

yukinko.title:
Lift 2
night sessions.
yukinko.lead: Night skiing on Course 2 in some seasons — extra runs after work or to avoid midday crowds. Check the dashboard for today.

family.title:
Gentle terrain,
easy to navigate.
family.lead: Mostly beginner–intermediate — the 1,500 m forest run averages about 6°. Two lifts, compact layout, popular with families.
family.noticeBody: Spring and warm afternoons can turn mushy — mornings are often best.
family.imgAlt: Families on gentle slopes at Sanokura

workation.title:
Kitakata base,
ski then soak.
workation.lead: Rent a car from Kitakata Station (~30 min) — ski Sanokura in the morning, ramen and Netsu-shio Onsen in the afternoon.

01.title: Kitakata Station
01.body: Ban-etsu West Line · rent a car

local.title: Aizu powder guide
local.body: Empty, affordable, north-facing — a model day loop around Sanokura without fighting Nekoma crowds.

schools.title: Forest run & ungroomed
schools.body: The 1,500 m forest course and main piste — where to find powder and why lines stay short.

summer.title: Sanokura off-season
summer.body: Sunflower fields and highland scenery — what to do around Mount Sanokura when the lifts are closed.

access.line: 857-1 Kita-Gongenmori Ko, Aida, Netsushio-Kano, Kitakata, Fukushima
access.note: ~17 km from JR Kitakata Station (~30 min by car) · no shuttle · free parking
access.rentacarLink: Book a rental car at Niigata Airport
access.rentacarHint: For Kitakata, Inawashiro, and Aizu-Wakamatsu drives
